assertions.expectation: Explains that surge upgrades require extra compute capacity (maxSurge creates NEW nodes before draining old ones)
assertions.reason: The response explains that surge nodes can't be created due to quota constraints and clearly describes how drain-first mode works as an alternative

assertions.expectation: Recommends setting maxUnavailable >0 instead of relying on maxSurge (drains first, then creates — uses less quota)
assertions.reason: Option 1 specifically recommends setting maxUnavailable to 1 and maxSurge to 0, explicitly describing this as a drain-first approach

assertions.expectation: Warns that maxUnavailable >0 means reduced capacity during upgrade — some pods won't have nodes
assertions.reason: The response warns about 'temporary capacity loss' and 'temporary capacity reduction' and mentions workloads need to 'tolerate this brief capacity dip'

assertions.expectation: Suggests temporarily scaling down non-critical deployments to free up quota for surge nodes
assertions.reason: Option 3 and the recommended approach both suggest scaling down non-critical workloads with specific kubectl commands provided

assertions.expectation: Mentions that reducing maxSurge to 1 minimizes extra quota needed (slower but fits within constraints)
assertions.reason: Option 2 demonstrates setting maxSurge to 1 and explains it 'creates only 1 surge node at a time instead of the default percentage-based surge'

assertions.expectation: Provides the gcloud command to update surge settings (maxSurge=0, maxUnavailable=1 for zero-extra-capacity mode)
assertions.reason: Multiple gcloud commands are provided showing exactly how to set maxSurge=0 and maxUnavailable=1

assertions.expectation: Recommends doing the upgrade in off-peak hours when fewer pods are running
assertions.reason: Option 4 specifically recommends scheduling during off-peak hours and explains the advantages of nights/weekends timing

assertions.expectation: Mentions requesting a temporary quota increase as a faster alternative to a permanent increase
assertions.reason: The response does not mention requesting a temporary quota increase as an alternative - it only provides workarounds for the existing quota constraint
